expectations.text: Uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsVforce* parameter), not Fortran-style flat params with trailing underscore
expectations.evidence: Uses Fortran-style: void vfosub_(int *id, double *time, double *par, int *npar, int *dflag, int *iflag, double *result)
No typedef. No forward declaration present.

expectations.text: Includes slv_c_utils.h as the primary SDK header
Includes iflag guard that skips iflag 5, 7, 9
expectations.evidence: Uses if (*iflag != 0) return; — skips all non-zero iflag values instead of specifically guarding 5, 7, 9.

expectations.text: Uses c_sysary or c_sysfnc to query displacement (not a raw function call)
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: c_sysfnc(func_name, ipar, nargs, &dx, &errflg); with func_name='DX'

expectations.text: Uses c_errmes for error handling after utility function calls
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: c_errmes(1, "Error in VFOSUB: could not evaluate DX", *id, "VFORCE");

expectations.text: Parameterizes markers or constants via PAR[] instead of hardcoding marker IDs in the function body
expectations.evidence: Hardcoded: ipar[0] = 10; ipar[1] = 1; and -500.0 * (dx - 100.0). Notes acknowledge hardcoding.

expectations.text: Build command uses correct library name adams_util.lib (not adams_utils.lib)
expectations.evidence: Uses wrong library: adams_solver_c.lib (not the correct name).

expectations.text: Build command uses mdi.bat (Windows) or mdi -c (Linux), not raw cl/gcc/ifort
expectations.evidence: Uses raw cl /LD on Windows; raw gcc -shared on Linux.

expectations.text: Build instructions mention setting up the Adams environment before running mdi.bat
expectations.evidence: Does not use mdi.bat; mentions adams_env.bat for env setup but with raw compiler commands.
